sentences.quote: We present the first formally verified anomaly constraint in particle physics: the Standard Model's global anomaly in $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$, computed from representation-theoretic data and machine-checked in Lean~4.
sentences.quote_normalized: we present the first formally verified anomaly constraint in particle physics: the standard model's global anomaly in $\mathbb{z}_{16}$, computed from representation-theoretic data and machine-checked in lean~4.
sentences.agent_notes: Priority/novelty meta-claim. Backed by Z16AnomalyComputation.lean (23 thms) + SMFermionData.lean (48 thms). Cannot verify 'first' globally but supporting Lean infrastructure is real.

sentences.quote: The discrete $\mathbb{Z}_4$ symmetry generated by $X = 5(B-L) - 4Y$ has a global anomaly classified by $\Omega_5^{\mathrm{Spin}^{\mathbb{Z}_4}} \cong \mathbb{Z}_{16}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the discrete $\mathbb{z}_4$ symmetry generated by $x = 5(b-l) - 4y$ has a global anomaly classified by $\omega_5^{spin^{\mathbb{z}_4}} \cong \mathbb{z}_{16}$.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard result from Garcia-Etxebarria & Montero (2019); citation present in bibliography.

sentences.quote: With right-handed neutrinos, the anomaly index is $16 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$ (anomaly-free); without, it is $15 \equiv -1 \pmod{16}$ (anomalous).
sentences.quote_normalized: with right-handed neutrinos, the anomaly index is $16 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$ (anomaly-free); without, it is $15 \equiv -1 \pmod{16}$ (anomalous).
sentences.agent_notes: Arithmetic: 6+3+3+2+1+1=16 (with nu_R), 6+3+3+2+1=15 (without). Both verified in SMFermionData.lean.

sentences.quote: Three generations without $\nu_R$ give anomaly $-3 \pmod{16}$, requiring either right-handed neutrinos or additional beyond-SM content to cancel the anomaly.
sentences.quote_normalized: three generations without $\nu_r$ give anomaly $-3 \pmod{16}$, requiring either right-handed neutrinos or additional beyond-sm content to cancel the anomaly.
sentences.agent_notes: 3*15=45 mod 16 = 13 = -3 mod 16. Verified.

sentences.quote: We also derive $N_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$ from modular invariance.
sentences.quote_normalized: we also derive $n_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$ from modular invariance.
sentences.agent_notes: GenerationConstraint.generation_mod3_constraint exists; theorem is conditional on modular invariance hypothesis (disclosed in Sec 3 line 148).

sentences.quote: Additionally, we construct the first monoidal category instance for $G$-graded modules in a proof assistant, compute the Drinfeld centers of $\mathrm{Vec}_{\mathbb{Z}/2}$ (toric code, 4 anyons) and $\mathrm{Vec}_{S_3}$ (8 non-abelian anyons), and establish $Z(\mathrm{Vec}_G)$ as a braided monoidal category via Mathlib's infrastructure.
sentences.quote_normalized: additionally, we construct the first monoidal category instance for $g$-graded modules in a proof assistant, compute the drinfeld centers of $vec_{\mathbb{z}/2}$ (toric code, 4 anyons) and $vec_{s_3}$ (8 non-abelian anyons), and establish $z(vec_g)$ as a braided monoidal category via mathlib's infrastructure.
sentences.agent_notes: Anyon counts (4 / 8) match S3CenterAnyons + ToricCodeCenter modules. 'First in proof assistant' is novelty claim.

sentences.quote: All modules referenced in this paper are verified by \texttt{lake build} with zero \texttt{sorry}.
sentences.quote_normalized: all modules referenced in this paper are verified by lake build with zero sorry.
sentences.agent_notes: All paper-9-referenced modules (SMFermionData, Z16AnomalyComputation, WangBridge, GenerationConstraint, VecGMonoidal, ToricCodeCenter, S3CenterAnyons, CenterEquivalenceZ2, DrinfeldDoubleAlgebra, DrinfeldDoubleRing, DrinfeldEquivalence, SPTStacking, KMatrixAnomaly, ModularInvarianceConstraint) are sorry-free per current grep (lean/SKEFTHawking/*.lean). Project-wide also 0 real sorries (matches in CenterFunctorZ2Equiv/KerrSchild/TetradGapEquation are inside comment blocks).

sentences.quote: The broader project comprises 1179~theorems across 79~Lean~4 modules (zero axioms), with 307 filled by the Aristotle automated prover.
sentences.quote_normalized: the broader project comprises 1179~theorems across 79~lean~4 modules (zero axioms), with 307 filled by the aristotle automated prover.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: paper says '1179 theorems / 79 modules / 0 axioms / 307 Aristotle'. Current state from lean_deps.json: 4049 theorems / 170 modules / 1 axiom. ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS registry has 322 entries. All four counts drift by large margins. Likely stale text from an earlier Phase.

sentences.quote: The Standard Model (SM) of particle physics has well-known perturbative anomaly cancellation: the sum of hypercharges vanishes within each generation~\cite{GrossJackiw1972}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the standard model (sm) of particle physics has well-known perturbative anomaly cancellation: the sum of hypercharges vanishes within each generation~.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard textbook claim; cite present in bibliography (Phys. Rev. D 6, 477).

sentences.quote: Less appreciated is the \emph{global} (non-perturbative) anomaly classified by the bordism group $\Omega_5^{\mathrm{Spin}^{\mathbb{Z}_4}} \cong \mathbb{Z}_{16}$~\cite{GarciaEtxebarria2019}.
sentences.quote_normalized: less appreciated is the global (non-perturbative) anomaly classified by the bordism group $\omega_5^{spin^{\mathbb{z}_4}} \cong \mathbb{z}_{16}$~.
sentences.agent_notes: Backed by GarciaEtxebarria-Montero JHEP 08, 003 (2019).

sentences.quote: This anomaly connects to the chirality wall in condensed matter~\cite{GioiaThorngrenPRL2026} and constrains the SM's generation count~\cite{Wang2024}.
sentences.quote_normalized: this anomaly connects to the chirality wall in condensed matter~ and constrains the sm's generation count~.
sentences.agent_notes: Both bibitems present.

sentences.quote: In this Letter, we present three results:
sentences.quote_normalized: in this letter, we present three results:

sentences.quote: The SM anomaly computation in $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ from fermion representation data, formally verified in Lean~4 (Sec.~\ref{sec:anomaly}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the sm anomaly computation in $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ from fermion representation data, formally verified in lean~4 (sec.~).
sentences.agent_notes: Forward reference to Sec 2; supported by Z16AnomalyComputation.lean.

sentences.quote: The generation constraint $N_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$ derived from axiomatized modular invariance (Sec.~\ref{sec:generation}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the generation constraint $n_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$ derived from axiomatized modular invariance (sec.~).
sentences.agent_notes: generation_mod3_constraint resolved.

sentences.quote: The first Drinfeld center computations in a proof assistant: $Z(\mathrm{Vec}_{\mathbb{Z}/2})$ (toric code) and $Z(\mathrm{Vec}_{S_3})$ (non-abelian), with $Z(\mathrm{Vec}_G)$ established as a braided monoidal category (Sec.~\ref{sec:drinfeld}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the first drinfeld center computations in a proof assistant: $z(vec_{\mathbb{z}/2})$ (toric code) and $z(vec_{s_3})$ (non-abelian), with $z(vec_g)$ established as a braided monoidal category (sec.~).
sentences.agent_notes: All three modules present.

sentences.quote: The same $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ that classifies the chirality wall also constrains the SM's generation count and requires either right-handed neutrinos or additional beyond-SM content to cancel the anomaly---all formally verified.
sentences.quote_normalized: the same $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ that classifies the chirality wall also constrains the sm's generation count and requires either right-handed neutrinos or additional beyond-sm content to cancel the anomaly---all formally verified.
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive synthesis sentence; constituent claims are verified elsewhere.

sentences.quote: The discrete $\mathbb{Z}_4$ symmetry is generated by~\cite{GarciaEtxebarria2019} \begin{equation} X = 5(B-L) - 4Y, \end{equation}
sentences.quote_normalized: the discrete $\mathbb{z}_4$ symmetry is generated by~ \begin{equation} x = 5(b-l) - 4y, \end{equation}
sentences.agent_notes: Definition of X; cited.

sentences.quote: where $B-L$ is baryon minus lepton number and $Y$ is weak hypercharge ($Q = T_3 + Y$). For each SM fermion multiplet (left-handed Weyl basis), $X \bmod 4$ determines the anomaly contribution.
sentences.quote_normalized: where $b-l$ is baryon minus lepton number and $y$ is weak hypercharge ($q = t_3 + y$). for each sm fermion multiplet (left-handed weyl basis), $x \bmod 4$ determines the anomaly contribution.

sentences.quote: All six SM fermion types have \emph{odd} $\mathbb{Z}_4$ charge ($X \equiv 1 \pmod{4}$), verified exhaustively in \texttt{SMFermionData.lean} (19 theorems, zero sorry).
sentences.quote_normalized: all six sm fermion types have odd $\mathbb{z}_4$ charge ($x \equiv 1 \pmod{4}$), verified exhaustively in smfermiondata.lean (19 theorems, zero sorry).

sentences.quote: Each left-handed Weyl component with odd charge contributes $+1$ to the anomaly index valued in $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: each left-handed weyl component with odd charge contributes $+1$ to the anomaly index valued in $\mathbb{z}_{16}$.

sentences.quote: The total per generation:
sentences.quote_normalized: the total per generation:

sentences.quote: With $\nu_R$: $6 + 3 + 3 + 2 + 1 + 1 = 16 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$ (anomaly-free).
sentences.quote_normalized: with $\nu_r$: $6 + 3 + 3 + 2 + 1 + 1 = 16 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$ (anomaly-free).

sentences.quote: Without $\nu_R$: $6 + 3 + 3 + 2 + 1 = 15 \equiv -1 \pmod{16}$ (anomalous).
sentences.quote_normalized: without $\nu_r$: $6 + 3 + 3 + 2 + 1 = 15 \equiv -1 \pmod{16}$ (anomalous).

sentences.quote: SM fermion anomaly contributions in $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$. Quarks (blue), leptons (amber), $\nu_R$ (green). The $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ boundary at 16 (dashed) is reached only with $\nu_R$.
sentences.quote_normalized: sm fermion anomaly contributions in $\mathbb{z}_{16}$. quarks (blue), leptons (amber), $\nu_r$ (green). the $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ boundary at 16 (dashed) is reached only with $\nu_r$.

sentences.quote: For three generations without $\nu_R$: $3 \times 15 = 45 \equiv -3 \pmod{16}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: for three generations without $\nu_r$: $3 \times 15 = 45 \equiv -3 \pmod{16}$.

sentences.quote: Since $-3 \not\equiv 0$, the three-generation SM without $\nu_R$ is anomalous and \emph{requires either right-handed neutrinos or additional beyond-SM content} to cancel the anomaly~\cite{GarciaEtxebarria2019}. This is proved as \texttt{hidden\_sector\_required} in \texttt{Z16AnomalyComputation.lean} (23 theorems, zero axioms, zero sorry; manual proof).
sentences.quote_normalized: since $-3 \not\equiv 0$, the three-generation sm without $\nu_r$ is anomalous and requires either right-handed neutrinos or additional beyond-sm content to cancel the anomaly~. this is proved as hidden\_sector\_required in z16anomalycomputation.lean (23 theorems, zero axioms, zero sorry; manual proof).
sentences.agent_notes: hidden_sector_required exists in Z16AnomalyComputation; module has 23 theorems and 0 axioms (matches paper). Manual-proof attribution is consistent with the prior review's note.

sentences.quote: $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ anomaly index vs.\ generation count. Blue circles: with $\nu_R$ (always 0). Amber diamonds: without $\nu_R$ (discrete $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ values). Dotted line: $N_f = 3$ (observed).
sentences.quote_normalized: $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ anomaly index vs.\ generation count. blue circles: with $\nu_r$ (always 0). amber diamonds: without $\nu_r$ (discrete $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ values). dotted line: $n_f = 3$ (observed).
sentences.agent_notes: Figure 2 caption.

sentences.quote: The number of SM generations is constrained by modular invariance.
sentences.quote_normalized: the number of sm generations is constrained by modular invariance.

sentences.quote: Each left-handed Weyl fermion contributes $c = 1/2$ to the 2D chiral central charge upon dimensional reduction~\cite{AlvarezGaumeWitten1984}.
sentences.quote_normalized: each left-handed weyl fermion contributes $c = 1/2$ to the 2d chiral central charge upon dimensional reduction~.

sentences.quote: The SM has 16 Weyl components per generation (Sec.~\ref{sec:anomaly}), giving $c_- = 16/2 = 8$ per generation, hence $c_- = 8 N_f$~\cite{Wang2024}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the sm has 16 weyl components per generation (sec.~), giving $c_- = 16/2 = 8$ per generation, hence $c_- = 8 n_f$~.

sentences.quote: The coefficient ``8'' is thus \emph{derived} from the SM fermion content, not a free parameter.
sentences.quote_normalized: the coefficient ``8'' is thus derived from the sm fermion content, not a free parameter.

sentences.quote: Notably, without $\nu_R$ one obtains $c_- = 15/2$ per generation---a fractional central charge signaling a gravitational anomaly and independently requiring $\nu_R$.
sentences.quote_normalized: notably, without $\nu_r$ one obtains $c_- = 15/2$ per generation---a fractional central charge signaling a gravitational anomaly and independently requiring $\nu_r$.
sentences.agent_notes: Backed by central_charge_fractional_without_nu_R.

sentences.quote: The framing anomaly requires $c_- \equiv 0 \pmod{24}$~\cite{AlvarezGaumeWitten1984,Stolz1993}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the framing anomaly requires $c_- \equiv 0 \pmod{24}$~.

sentences.quote: Together: \begin{equation} 8 N_f \equiv 0 \pmod{24} \quad \Longrightarrow \quad N_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}. \end{equation} $N_f = 3$ is the minimal nontrivial solution.
sentences.quote_normalized: together: \begin{equation} 8 n_f \equiv 0 \pmod{24} \quad \longrightarrow \quad n_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}. \end{equation} $n_f = 3$ is the minimal nontrivial solution.
sentences.agent_notes: gcd(8,24)=8, 24/8=3. Lean theorem confirms.

sentences.quote: The bridge from fermion content to generation constraint is formalized in \texttt{WangBridge.lean} (9 theorems, zero sorry): the theorem \texttt{fermion\_count\_gives\_central\_charge} derives $c_- = 8$ from \texttt{SMFermionData.total\_components\_with\_nu\_R}, and \texttt{central\_charge\_fractional\_without\_nu\_R} proves that 15/2 is not an integer ($\nu_R$ required).
sentences.quote_normalized: the bridge from fermion content to generation constraint is formalized in wangbridge.lean (9 theorems, zero sorry): the theorem fermion\_count\_gives\_central\_charge derives $c_- = 8$ from smfermiondata.total\_components\_with\_nu\_r, and central\_charge\_fractional\_without\_nu\_r proves that 15/2 is not an integer ($\nu_r$ required).
sentences.agent_notes: IA: paper says WangBridge.lean has '9 theorems'; lean_deps.json shows 10 theorems. Three named refs all resolve. Theorem-name claims PASS; only the count drifts.

sentences.quote: The algebraic constraint is in \texttt{GenerationConstraint.lean} (13 theorems, zero sorry, zero axioms): \texttt{generation\_mod3\_constraint} is a conditional \emph{if} $24 \mid 8 N_f$ \emph{then} $3 \mid N_f$, proved by Aristotle (run \texttt{a1dfcbde}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the algebraic constraint is in generationconstraint.lean (13 theorems, zero sorry, zero axioms): generation\_mod3\_constraint is a conditional if $24 \mid 8 n_f$ then $3 \mid n_f$, proved by aristotle (run a1dfcbde).
sentences.agent_notes: GenerationConstraint module has 13 theorems / 0 axioms (matches). Aristotle run a1dfcbde present in ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS registry.

sentences.quote: The modular invariance hypothesis enters as a premise, not an axiom.
sentences.quote_normalized: the modular invariance hypothesis enters as a premise, not an axiom.
sentences.agent_notes: Conditional theorem (24 | 8 N_f -> 3 | N_f) discloses the hypothesis as a premise.

sentences.quote: Generation constraint: $c_- = 8 N_f \equiv 0 \pmod{24}$. Blue bars: $N_f$ divisible by 3. Grey bars: excluded. Dashed lines at $c_- = 24, 48, 72$.
sentences.quote_normalized: generation constraint: $c_- = 8 n_f \equiv 0 \pmod{24}$. blue bars: $n_f$ divisible by 3. grey bars: excluded. dashed lines at $c_- = 24, 48, 72$.

sentences.quote: We define $\mathrm{Vec}_G$ as \texttt{GradedObject (Additive G) (ModuleCat k)} using Mathlib's graded object infrastructure. The monoidal structure (Day convolution) is proved via typeclass synthesis: \texttt{MonoidalCategory (VecG\_Cat k G)} synthesizes from Mathlib's \texttt{GradedObject.monoidalCategory} instance (\texttt{VecGMonoidal.lean}, 12 theorems, zero sorry).
sentences.quote_normalized: we define $vec_g$ as gradedobject (additive g) (modulecat k) using mathlib's graded object infrastructure. the monoidal structure (day convolution) is proved via typeclass synthesis: monoidalcategory (vecg\_cat k g) synthesizes from mathlib's gradedobject.monoidalcategory instance (vecgmonoidal.lean, 12 theorems, zero sorry).

sentences.quote: The Drinfeld center $Z(\mathrm{Vec}_G)$ is then automatically a braided monoidal category via Mathlib's \texttt{Center} construction. The braided structure requires $G$ commutative (\texttt{CommGroup G}), resolved by Aristotle (run \texttt{48493889}) using a separate section to avoid typeclass diamonds.
sentences.quote_normalized: the drinfeld center $z(vec_g)$ is then automatically a braided monoidal category via mathlib's center construction. the braided structure requires $g$ commutative (commgroup g), resolved by aristotle (run 48493889) using a separate section to avoid typeclass diamonds.

sentences.quote: The $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ classification of fermionic SPT phases in 3+1D has a natural group structure under ``stacking'': placing two SPT slabs side by side.
sentences.quote_normalized: the $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ classification of fermionic spt phases in 3+1d has a natural group structure under ``stacking'': placing two spt slabs side by side.

sentences.quote: We formalize this group structure exactly in \lean{SPTStacking.lean}, modeling SPT phases as elements of $\mathbb{Z}/16\mathbb{Z}$ with all group axioms (associativity, commutativity, identity, inverse) proved by \texttt{decide} over Mathlib's \lean{ZMod 16}.
sentences.quote_normalized: we formalize this group structure exactly in sptstacking.lean, modeling spt phases as elements of $\mathbb{z}/16\mathbb{z}$ with all group axioms (associativity, commutativity, identity, inverse) proved by decide over mathlib's zmod 16.
sentences.agent_notes: SPTStacking module exists with 29 theorems / 69 total decls / 0 axioms / sorry-free.

sentences.quote: \textbf{SM fermion stacking.} One SM generation contributes 16 Majorana fermions, corresponding to the identity element $0 \in \mathbb{Z}_{16}$: anomaly-free by construction. Three generations ($48 = 3 \times 16 \equiv 0$) are likewise trivial. Without $\nu_R$, each generation contributes 15 Majorana ($\equiv -1 \bmod 16$), so three generations give $45 \equiv 13 \bmod 16 \neq 0$: a genuine anomaly obstruction. Both results verified by \texttt{decide}.
sentences.quote_normalized: sm fermion stacking. one sm generation contributes 16 majorana fermions, corresponding to the identity element $0 \in \mathbb{z}_{16}$: anomaly-free by construction. three generations ($48 = 3 \times 16 \equiv 0$) are likewise trivial. without $\nu_r$, each generation contributes 15 majorana ($\equiv -1 \bmod 16$), so three generations give $45 \equiv 13 \bmod 16 \neq 0$: a genuine anomaly obstruction. both results verified by decide.
sentences.agent_notes: Arithmetic verified: 48 mod 16 = 0; 45 mod 16 = 13.

sentences.quote: \textbf{The 16-fold periodicity.} 16 copies of a single Majorana fermion can be gapped by interactions (proved: stacking 16 copies of the generator gives the identity). 8 copies \emph{cannot} be gapped (proved: $8 \neq 0$ in $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$). This is the Fidkowski-Kitaev result~\cite{fidkowski2010} elevated to 3+1D.
sentences.quote_normalized: the 16-fold periodicity. 16 copies of a single majorana fermion can be gapped by interactions (proved: stacking 16 copies of the generator gives the identity). 8 copies cannot be gapped (proved: $8 \neq 0$ in $\mathbb{z}_{16}$). this is the fidkowski-kitaev result~ elevated to 3+1d.

sentences.quote: \textbf{Anomaly matching and interfaces.} Two SPT phases can be connected by a gapped interface if and only if their anomaly indices match: $\nu(P_1) = \nu(P_2)$ iff $P_1 + (-P_2) = 0$ in $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ (proved). This is the mathematical content of the TPF conjecture~\cite{tpf2026}: when anomalies match, the stacked system is trivial, so a gapped boundary exists.
sentences.quote_normalized: anomaly matching and interfaces. two spt phases can be connected by a gapped interface if and only if their anomaly indices match: $\nu(p_1) = \nu(p_2)$ iff $p_1 + (-p_2) = 0$ in $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ (proved). this is the mathematical content of the tpf conjecture~: when anomalies match, the stacked system is trivial, so a gapped boundary exists.

sentences.quote: The module also formalizes the 1+1D analog ($\mathbb{Z}_8$ classification) and verifies anomaly matching for the 3450 model ($25 \equiv 1 \bmod 8$ for both left and right sectors), connecting to our K-matrix results in \lean{KMatrixAnomaly.lean}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the module also formalizes the 1+1d analog ($\mathbb{z}_8$ classification) and verifies anomaly matching for the 3450 model ($25 \equiv 1 \bmod 8$ for both left and right sectors), connecting to our k-matrix results in kmatrixanomaly.lean.
sentences.agent_notes: 25 mod 8 = 1. Both modules exist and are sorry-free.

sentences.quote: We have established:
sentences.quote_normalized: we have established:

sentences.quote: The \emph{first formally verified anomaly constraint} in particle physics: the SM's $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ anomaly computed from representation data.
sentences.quote_normalized: the first formally verified anomaly constraint in particle physics: the sm's $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ anomaly computed from representation data.
sentences.agent_notes: Priority/novelty repeat of abstract claim.

sentences.quote: The generation constraint $N_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$ derived from axiomatized modular invariance, with $N_f = 3$ as the minimal nontrivial solution.
sentences.quote_normalized: the generation constraint $n_f \equiv 0 \pmod{3}$ derived from axiomatized modular invariance, with $n_f = 3$ as the minimal nontrivial solution.

sentences.quote: The \emph{first Drinfeld center computations} in a proof assistant: toric code (4 abelian anyons) and $D(S_3)$ (8 non-abelian anyons).
sentences.quote_normalized: the first drinfeld center computations in a proof assistant: toric code (4 abelian anyons) and $d(s_3)$ (8 non-abelian anyons).

sentences.quote: $Z(\mathrm{Vec}_G)$ as a braided monoidal category via Mathlib's \texttt{Center} infrastructure --- the first such construction connecting abstract category theory to concrete gauge emergence.
sentences.quote_normalized: $z(vec_g)$ as a braided monoidal category via mathlib's center infrastructure --- the first such construction connecting abstract category theory to concrete gauge emergence.

sentences.quote: $D(G)$ as a Mathlib \texttt{Ring} and $k$-\texttt{Algebra}, with Hopf algebra structure (coproduct, counit, involutive antipode), establishing the algebraic foundation for $Z(\mathrm{Vec}_G) \cong \mathrm{Rep}(D(G))$.
sentences.quote_normalized: $d(g)$ as a mathlib ring and $k$-algebra, with hopf algebra structure (coproduct, counit, involutive antipode), establishing the algebraic foundation for $z(vec_g) \cong rep(d(g))$.

sentences.quote: The formal verification comprises 1179 theorems across 79 Lean~4 modules, with zero axioms. All modules referenced in this paper have zero \texttt{sorry}; 307 theorems are filled by the Aristotle automated prover (304 machine-proved, 3 manual) across 35+ runs.
sentences.quote_normalized: the formal verification comprises 1179 theorems across 79 lean~4 modules, with zero axioms. all modules referenced in this paper have zero sorry; 307 theorems are filled by the aristotle automated prover (304 machine-proved, 3 manual) across 35+ runs.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: paper says '1179 theorems / 79 modules / 0 axioms / 307 Aristotle (304 machine + 3 manual)'. Current state from lean_deps.json: 4049 theorems / 170 modules / 1 axiom (`SKEFTHawking.GoltermanShamir` namespace). ARISTOTLE_THEOREMS registry has 322 entries. Counts drift heavily; '0 axioms' is also wrong now (1 axiom present). 'All modules referenced in this paper have zero sorry' is independently true.

sentences.quote: The broader project includes the first Hopf algebra, quantum group fusion rules, and modular tensor category definitions in any proof assistant.
sentences.quote_normalized: the broader project includes the first hopf algebra, quantum group fusion rules, and modular tensor category definitions in any proof assistant.
sentences.agent_notes: Priority/novelty claim about broader project; not directly verifiable by structural recheck.

sentences.quote: The same $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ that classifies the chirality wall~\cite{GioiaThorngrenPRL2026} also constrains the SM's generation count, requiring either right-handed neutrinos or additional beyond-SM content to cancel the anomaly, consistent with Garcia-Etxebarria and Montero~\cite{GarciaEtxebarria2019}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the same $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ that classifies the chirality wall~ also constrains the sm's generation count, requiring either right-handed neutrinos or additional beyond-sm content to cancel the anomaly, consistent with garcia-etxebarria and montero~.

sentences.quote: The Drinfeld center bridge connects microscopic categorical data to macroscopic gauge emergence through Mathlib's verified infrastructure, establishing a formally verified path from topological order to gauge theory.
sentences.quote_normalized: the drinfeld center bridge connects microscopic categorical data to macroscopic gauge emergence through mathlib's verified infrastructure, establishing a formally verified path from topological order to gauge theory.
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive synthesis closing sentence.

sentences.quote: Lean~4 proofs verified by \texttt{lake build} (v4.28.0, Mathlib \texttt{8f9d9cff}).
sentences.quote_normalized: lean~4 proofs verified by lake build (v4.28.0, mathlib 8f9d9cff).
sentences.agent_notes: TP: paper says 'Lean v4.28.0, Mathlib 8f9d9cff'; current lean-toolchain is 'leanprover/lean4:v4.29.0' and lakefile.toml has Mathlib rev '8850ed93863d6ce27210a673f9ed235a4e8a2994'. BOTH pins drift. FAIL.

sentences.quote: Automated proofs by the Aristotle theorem prover (Harmonic).
sentences.quote_normalized: automated proofs by the aristotle theorem prover (harmonic).
sentences.agent_notes: Acknowledgment line; trivially true.
